A confirmed tornado touched down northeast of Mondovi in Buffalo County then lifted after briefly crossing into southern Eau Claire County. Some structure and tree damage was noted. Time estimated from radar.

Corrects previous tornado report from 10 SW Fall Creek. A confirmed tornado briefly touched down just south of Cleghorn and west of Foster. Multiple pine trees were uprooted and twisted. Time estimated from radar.
Structure damage and photos confirm a tornado in southern Eau Claire County. The path may stretch to east of Augusta. Time estimated from radar.
A brief tornado was confirmed near Hale Corner with minor tree and structure damage noted. Time estimated from radar.
A confirmed tornado touched down south of Augusta and traveled northeast across US-12 before dissipating north of Fairchild. Both structure and tree damage were noted. Time estimated from radar.

This tornado hit a barn and tore off part of its roof. It also damaged a door. Several trees were broken. Its path length was 0.8 mile and the maximum width was 25 yards. This tornado is separate from another tornado six miles southeast of Augusta at 813 PM.

Corrects previous tornado report from 3 SE Augusta. A confirmed tornado touched down southeast of Augusta near the intersection of Bluff Creek Rd and County Rd M and traveled east-northeast before dissipating a tenth of a mile past the intersection of County Rd H and Deer Ln. Both structure and tree damage were noted. Time estimated from radar.

Video showed ground circulation at first, then became fully condensed. The tornado mostly moved across open fields, but it did hit some groves of trees, knocking down large branches. It hit one farm and took some tin off a shed. It was rated EF-0 with a maximum estimated wind of 75 mph. Path length was 4.2 miles and maximum width was 25 yards. It was on the ground for nine minutes and was very narrow and rope like toward the end.
Location estimated based on spotter report. Time is approximate. Photo provided.

Video showed ground circulation. The tornado moved across a grove of trees and knocked down large branches. Rated EF-0 with maximum winds estimated at 75 mph. Path length 0.5 miles and maximum width 40 yards.
